name: King Arthur Pendragon
shortDescription: King Arthur is a legendary British leader who, according to medieval histories and romances, led the defence of Britain against Saxon invaders in the late 5th and early 6th centuries.
description: King Arthur Pendragon is a central figure in the legends surrounding the island of Great Britain. He is primarily known as a legendary king who defended Britain against Saxon invaders. The details of Arthur's story vary widely depending on the source, but common elements include his miraculous birth, often involving Uther Pendragon and Igraine, aided by Merlin. He is raised in secrecy and later proves his right to the throne by drawing the sword Excalibur from a stone (or being given it by the Lady of the Lake). Arthur establishes a kingdom at Camelot, where he gathers the Knights of the Round Table, a fellowship of valiant warriors. His quests include battling monstrous creatures, fighting rebellious lords, and seeking the Holy Grail. Arthur's reign is marked by chivalry, justice, and prosperity, but ultimately ends in tragedy due to the treachery of Mordred, often portrayed as Arthur's illegitimate son or nephew. Arthur is mortally wounded in the Battle of Camlann and taken to the Isle of Avalon, where he awaits the day he will return to save Britain. The historicity of Arthur is debated, with some scholars believing he was a real Romano-British war leader, while others consider him purely a figure of myth and legend.
quests: Drawing the sword from the stone
Establishing the Knights of the Round Table
The Quest for the Holy Grail

sources.title: The History of the Kings of Britain
sources.author: Geoffrey of Monmouth
